# Containers {#sec-containers}

Savio does not allow users to install system-level libraries or run Docker directly. Instead, we build Docker images locally (or in CI), push them to a registry, and pull them on Savio as Apptainer (formerly Singularity) images. This gives us full control over our software stack while respecting the shared HPC environment.

## Overview of the workflow

```
Local machine                    Docker Hub              Savio
─────────────                    ──────────              ─────
docker build → docker push  →  registry  →  apptainer pull → .sif file
```

1. **Build** a Docker image on your laptop or a CI runner.
2. **Push** it to Docker Hub (or another registry).
3. **Pull** it on Savio with `apptainer pull`, which converts it to a `.sif` file.
4. **Run** R sessions, scripts, or SLURM jobs inside the container.

## Building a Docker image

### Example Dockerfile

Below is the Dockerfile the lab uses for the climate–malaria project. It starts from the [`rocker/geospatial`](https://rocker-project.org/) base image, which ships with R, `sf`, `terra`, `raster`, `rgdal`, `ncdf4`, and all their system dependencies (GDAL, PROJ, GEOS, netCDF, HDF5).

```{.dockerfile filename="Dockerfile"}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# R 4.2.3 + rgdal + velox + tidyverse + HPC utilities
#
# Build:   docker build --platform linux/amd64 -t cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3 .
# Push:    docker push cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3
# Savio:   apptainer pull docker://cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
FROM rocker/geospatial:4.2.3

# System deps for velox (Boost geometry headers)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libboost-all-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# CRAN packages (pinned to a Posit snapshot for reproducibility)
RUN R -e " \
    options(repos = c(CRAN = 'https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2023-04-20')); \
    install.packages(c( \
        'tidyverse',     \
        'here',          \
        'reshape',       \
        'data.table',    \
        'feather',       \
        'progressr',     \
        'future.apply'   \
    )) \
"

# velox — archived on CRAN, so install from GitHub
RUN R -e " \
    options(repos = c(CRAN = 'https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2023-04-20')); \
    install.packages('remotes'); \
    remotes::install_github('hunzikp/velox', upgrade = 'never') \
"

# Smoke test — fail the build if anything is missing
RUN R -e " \
    library(rgdal); library(velox); library(ncdf4); library(raster); \
    library(sp); library(sf); library(tidyverse); library(here); \
    library(reshape); library(data.table); library(feather); \
    library(progressr); library(future.apply); \
    cat('All packages OK\n') \
"

CMD ["R"]
```

### Build and push

You must build for `linux/amd64` since Savio runs x86_64 Linux, even if your laptop is Apple Silicon:

```bash
docker build --platform linux/amd64 -t cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3 .
docker push cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Pin your image to a tag (e.g., `4.2.3`) rather than using `latest`. This makes it easy to reproduce results months later.
:::

## Pulling the image on Savio

On Savio, convert the Docker image to an Apptainer `.sif` file:

```bash
apptainer pull docker://cmolitor/r-malaria-cru:4.2.3
```

Move the resulting `.sif` file to the shared container directory so other lab members can use it:

```bash
mv r-malaria-cru_4.2.3.sif \
    /global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/carleton_colab/software/apptainers/
```

## Running containers on Savio

All examples below assume the `.sif` file lives at:

```
/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/carleton_colab/software/apptainers/r-malaria-cru_4.2.3.sif
```

For readability, we define a shell variable:

```bash
SIF=/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/carleton_colab/software/apptainers/r-malaria-cru_4.2.3.sif
```

### Key Apptainer flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--cleanenv` | Do not pass host environment variables into the container. Prevents library conflicts. |
| `--contain` | Isolate the container from the host filesystem (only explicitly bound paths are visible). |
| `--bind A:B` | Mount host path `A` at container path `B` (or at `A` if `B` is omitted). |
| `--pwd /path` | Set the working directory inside the container. |

### Interactive R session {#sec-interactive-r}

First request a compute node (see @sec-daily-workflow), then:

```bash
apptainer exec --cleanenv --contain \
    --bind /global/scratch/projects/co_carleton:/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton \
    --bind /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum:/global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    --pwd /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    $SIF R
```

This drops you into an R console with your project directory as the working directory. The `here` package will anchor to `falciparum/` if you have a `.here` sentinel file there:

```bash
touch ~/falciparum/.here
```

### Running an R script {#sec-rscript}

To run a single script non-interactively:

```bash
apptainer exec --cleanenv --contain \
    --bind /global/scratch/projects/co_carleton:/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton \
    --bind /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum:/global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    --pwd /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    $SIF Rscript scripts/B01_Predict_Prevalence.R
```

### SLURM batch jobs {#sec-slurm}

For production runs, submit a SLURM script that wraps the Apptainer call:

```{.bash filename="run_bootstrap.sh"}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=malaria_bootstrap
#SBATCH --account=co_carleton
#SBATCH --partition=savio4_htc
#SBATCH --qos=carleton_htc4_normal
#SBATCH --time=08:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=12
#SBATCH --mem=64G
#SBATCH --output=logs/bootstrap_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=logs/bootstrap_%j.err

SIF=/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/carleton_colab/software/apptainers/r-malaria-cru_4.2.3.sif

apptainer exec --cleanenv --contain \
    --bind /global/scratch/projects/co_carleton:/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton \
    --bind /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum:/global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    --pwd /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    $SIF Rscript scripts/E01_Bootstrap.R
```

Submit with:

```bash
mkdir -p logs
sbatch run_bootstrap.sh
```

### SLURM job arrays {#sec-job-arrays}

Job arrays are ideal when you need to run the same script across many iterations (e.g., 1,000 bootstrap replicates). SLURM sets the `SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID` environment variable, which your R script can read to determine which iteration to run.

```{.bash filename="run_bootstrap_array.sh"}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=bootstrap_array
#SBATCH --account=co_carleton
#SBATCH --partition=savio4_htc
#SBATCH --qos=carleton_htc4_normal
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=32G
#SBATCH --array=1-1000%50
#SBATCH --output=logs/bootstrap_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=logs/bootstrap_%A_%a.err

SIF=/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/carleton_colab/software/apptainers/r-malaria-cru_4.2.3.sif

apptainer exec --cleanenv --contain \
    --bind /global/scratch/projects/co_carleton:/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton \
    --bind /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum:/global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    --pwd /global/home/users/$USER/falciparum \
    --env SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID=$SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID \
    $SIF Rscript scripts/E01_Bootstrap.R
```

The `--array=1-1000%50` directive creates 1,000 tasks but limits concurrency to 50 at a time. Inside R, read the task ID with:

```r
task_id <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID"))
```

::: {.callout-note}
Because `--cleanenv` strips host environment variables, you need to explicitly pass `SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID` into the container with `--env`.
:::

## Troubleshooting

### `here()` starts at the wrong directory

Make sure you are passing `--pwd` to set the working directory, and that the target path is also `--bind` mounted. Placing a `.here` file in your project root guarantees that the `here` package finds the right anchor point.

### Packages not found inside the container

If R cannot find a package, it is not installed in the `.sif` image. Add it to the Dockerfile, rebuild, push, and pull a new `.sif`. Container images are read-only on Savio — you cannot install packages into a running container.

### Parallel workers fail with `--contain`

With `--contain`, forked workers may not see paths that are not explicitly bound. Make sure every path your R scripts read from or write to is included in a `--bind` flag.

### `future` workers crash in Positron / Ark

If using `future` parallelism inside a Positron IDE session connected over SSH, the Ark kernel's `.ark_auto_step` function can leak into forked workers and cause errors. A workaround is to unset it before launching workers:

```r
if (exists(".ark_auto_step", envir = globalenv())) {
    rm(".ark_auto_step", envir = globalenv())
}

plan(multicore, workers = 12)
```
